disputed.description: Normalization-before-match is a well-known bypass class for anchored sanitizers. NFKC + trimStart on a probe (while logging the original) is low-cost and closes leading-whitespace/Unicode-lookalike evasion against the exact invariant the patch protects. High ROI; standard defensive pattern.

Tightening JSON-envelope detection to tolerate whitespace variants is cheap and directly addresses leak shapes that anchored literals miss (pretty-printed, spaced colons, prefixed 'Error:'). Pairs naturally with IMP-001's normalized probe. Some care needed to avoid over-matching legitimate in-character JSON-looking content, but at the chat-medium boundary the risk is low.

Regression tests are the cheapest durable defense for a BLOCKER-class invariant. Without locked tests for whitespace/multiline/spaced-JSON/NFKC variants, IMP-001/002 can silently regress. Tight, mechanical, high-leverage.

Boundary-level enforcement at outbound primitives is architecturally sound and prevents call-site drift as new write surfaces are added. Real value, but higher refactor cost than the regex/test fixes and requires care to avoid double-sanitization or breaking non-chat paths. Worth doing but not as urgent as closing the bypass itself.

Scoped spies are better hygiene than global console monkey-patching and reduce flake risk under parallel test runs. Real but lower-priority quality improvement; doesn't address the core invariant. Reasonable to bundle with the test additions in IMP-003.

blockers.concern: Anchor-bypass remains possible via zero-width/format control characters because matching only uses `trimStart().normalize('NFKC')` and does not strip Cf/Cc characters.
blockers.why_matters: A raw upstream error prefixed with invisible controls (for example zero-width characters) can evade `^`-anchored patterns and leak non-persona error text to Discord.
blockers.location: packages/persona-engine/src/deliver/sanitize.ts (probe construction and anchored pattern matching, around lines 389-429 and 503)
blockers.recommendation: Add a pre-match canonicalization step that removes leading and internal invisible format/control characters used for obfuscation (at least `\u200B-\u200D`, `\u2060`, `\uFEFF`, and related Cf/Cc classes where safe), then add regression tests for those bypass strings.

blockers.concern: Framing claims this PR adds `trimStart()` probe, NFKC normalization, and a widened `raw-json-error-envelope` regex (`\{\s*"type"\s*:\s*"error"`), but the diff shows NONE of these. The regex at sanitize.ts is still `/^(?:Error: )?\{"type":"error"/` (unchanged), `sanitizeOutboundBody` body contains no `trimStart()` or `String.prototype.normalize('NFKC')` call, and there is no probe-vs-substitute split. Either the diff is incomplete or the cover-letter overstates what landed. As-is, BLOCKER #1 from the prior gemini-skeptic pass (leading-whitespace + spaced-JSON bypass) is NOT closed by this PR.
blockers.why_matters: Reviewers (and autonomous gates) read the cover letter to decide whether prior BLOCKERs are remediated. If HIGH_CONSENSUS auto-integrates on the framing alone, an unfixed BLOCKER ships to main with a misleading commit trail. This also poisons the audit chain for bug-20260511-b6eb97.
blockers.location: PR cover letter vs `packages/persona-engine/src/deliver/sanitize.ts:354-489` (RAW_API_ERROR_PATTERNS + sanitizeOutboundBody body)
blockers.recommendation: Either (a) regenerate the diff to include the claimed trimStart+NFKC+widened-regex changes before merging, or (b) edit the cover letter to remove false claims and re-run flatline against the actual delta. Do NOT proceed on the current PR contents while the cover letter is misaligned.

blockers.concern:
Leading-whitespace bypass remains open. All 9 patterns are anchored with `^` and a literal token sequence; none strips or tolerates leading `
`, `
`, space, or tab. An LLM completion (or upstream rethrown error) that begins with a stray newline — e.g. `
API Error: 500 …` — does not match `^(?:Error: )?API Error: \d+` and ships raw to Discord. The prior pass identified this exact class (gemini-skeptic 735); the diff contains no defense.
blockers.why_matters: This is the specific bypass class the previous flatline blocked on. Leaving it open means the in-character-only invariant is still circumventable at the wire, and the bug-20260511-b6eb97 closure narrative is technically false.
blockers.location: `packages/persona-engine/src/deliver/sanitize.ts:475-489` (sanitizeOutboundBody — pattern loop with no leading-whitespace handling)
blockers.recommendation: Add a probe-trim step before pattern matching: `const probe = content.replace(/^[\s\uFEFF]+/, '')` and run regex.test(probe) — or strip leading whitespace from `content` before the loop. Add tests for `'\nAPI Error: 500'`, `'  API Error: 500'`, `'\tInternal Server Error'`, `'\r\n{"type":"error"…}'`. None of these exist in the new tests.

blockers.concern: Spaced-JSON envelope bypass remains open. `raw-json-error-envelope` regex is `/^(?:Error: )?\{"type":"error"/` — requires no whitespace between `{`, `"type"`, `:`, `"error"`. A pretty-printed or `JSON.stringify(obj, null, 2)` envelope (`'{\n  "type": "error",\n  …}'` or `'{ "type": "error" }'`) does not match. The framing claims this was widened to `\{\s*"type"\s*:\s*"error"` — but the diff disagrees.
blockers.why_matters: Any upstream code path that re-serializes the error body with non-zero indent (common in error-formatting middleware, stack-trace serializers, debugger output piped to user) bypasses the only pattern that targets the raw JSON shape.
blockers.recommendation: Apply the widening the cover letter already promises: `/^(?:Error: )?\{\s*"type"\s*:\s*"error"/`. Add tests for pretty-printed envelope and `{ "type" : "error" }` spaced variants.

blockers.concern: The regex for matching a raw JSON error envelope does not account for whitespace, making it brittle.
blockers.why_matters:
The PR description states the JSON regex was widened to handle spaced JSON (e.g., `{
  "type": "error"...`), but the implementation `regex: /^(?:Error: )?{"type":"error"/` does not allow any whitespace. Upstream services can and do format JSON with whitespace, which would bypass this pattern and leak the raw JSON error to the user.
blockers.recommendation: Update the `raw-json-error-envelope` regex to be whitespace-tolerant, as described in the PR context. A more robust pattern would be `/^(?:Error: )?\{\s*"type"\s*:\s*"error"/`.

blockers.concern: The sanitizer implementation is vulnerable to anchor bypass via leading whitespace and lacks Unicode normalization, contradicting the stated fix.
blockers.why_matters: The PR description claims the fix involves `trimStart()` and `NFKC-normalize`, but these are absent in the `sanitizeOutboundBody` implementation. All regex patterns are anchored with `^`, so a simple leading space or newline will defeat the sanitizer, causing the raw error to leak. This completely undermines the primary defense and fails to fix the original blocker.
blockers.recommendation: Modify `sanitizeOutboundBody` to apply `trimStart()` and `normalize('NFKC')` to the input `content` before testing the regex patterns. The start of the function should look like: `if (!content) return content; const probe = content.normalize('NFKC').trimStart(); ... for (const { name, regex } of RAW_API_ERROR_PATTERNS) { if (regex.test(probe)) { ... } }`.

blockers.concern: Unicode lookalike / homoglyph bypass open. No `String.prototype.normalize('NFKC')` is applied before pattern matching. A fullwidth-letter raw error (e.g. `'ＡＰＩ Error: 500 …'`, U+FF21 / U+FF30 / U+FF29) — which Discord still renders as visually-identical 'API Error' — does NOT match any anchored regex. Cover letter claims NFKC normalization landed; diff shows it did not. This is a real class because some logging middleware double-encodes or some characters arrive via copy-paste from upstream traces.
blockers.why_matters: The L7 / soul-identity-doc work explicitly closed this same Unicode-normalization bypass class (cycle-098 sprint-7 cypherpunk HIGH-2 in CLAUDE.md). The same defense pattern is missing here. Cross-primitive consistency matters for the chat-medium presentation boundary as a doctrine.
blockers.recommendation: Probe through `content.normalize('NFKC')` and strip Cf-category zero-width chars before regex testing. Mirror the L7 closure: `const probe = content.normalize('NFKC').replace(/[\u200B-\u200F\uFEFF\u2060]/g, '').replace(/^\s+/, '')`. Test fullwidth + zero-width-inserted variants.

blockers.concern: `discord-js-api-error` and `discord-js-http-error` patterns are too strict (`DiscordAPIError:` / `HTTPError:` only) and likely miss common bracketed forms.
blockers.why_matters: If discord.js emits `DiscordAPIError[50013]: ...` or `HTTPError[403]: ...`, sanitizer will not substitute and raw operational errors can still leak.
blockers.recommendation: Widen patterns to support bracketed variants (for example `DiscordAPIError(?:\[[^\]]+\])?:` and `HTTPError(?:\[[^\]]+\])?:`) and add explicit tests for both colon-only and bracketed signatures.
